description: Standard: news report heavy on passive voice

input.draft: A new regulation requiring emissions testing for all commercial vehicles was approved by the state legislature on Monday. The regulation was drafted in response to growing air quality concerns raised by health advocates. Under the new law, vehicles will be required to pass inspection annually. The cost of inspections will be borne by fleet operators. The measure was opposed by the trucking industry, which argued that compliance would be costly. Fines for non-compliance will be set at $500 per vehicle.
input.tolerance: Flag all passives, distinguish justified from problematic

input.draft: The decision was made last Tuesday.

description: European: policy article with justified passives

input.draft: The directive was transposed into national law by 24 of 27 member states by the December deadline. Three states — Poland, Hungary, and Romania — were granted extensions by the Commission. The regulation is expected to reduce industrial emissions by 40 percent by 2030. Environmental groups have welcomed the measure but argue that enforcement mechanisms remain underfunded. Penalties for non-compliance were set at €100,000 per violation, a figure described by industry as punitive.
